pubmed_links: [Ng et al. (2018) Biallelic mutations in FUT8 cause a congenital disorder of glycosylation with defective fucosylation](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/29304374/); [Ng et al. (2020) Expanding the molecular and clinical phenotypes of FUT8-CDG](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/32049367/)
summary: Biallelic loss-of-function variants in FUT8, encoding the enzyme responsible for core fucosylation of N-linked glycoproteins, cause FUT8-CDG, a congenital disorder of glycosylation characterized by severe intellectual disability, developmental delay, microcephaly, and seizures. (emerging evidence)
